Title: Metabolic modeling of microbial communities: Past, present, and future
Authors: Imen Tanniche, Bahareh Behkam
Quote: Incorporating both biochemical and biophysical temporal changes in environmental conditions would be advantageous for advancing metabolic models by integrating machine learning with genome-scale models to better understand complex biological relationships in microbial communities.

Title: Flux Balance Analysis for Media Optimization and Genetic Targets to Improve Heterologous Siderophore Production
Authors: Girish Swayambhu, Nicholas Moscatello, G Ekin Atilla-Gokcumen, Blaine A Pfeifer
Quote: FBA allows us to constrain the media components within a stoichiometric model, enabling computational prediction of enhanced media compositions before experimental validation

Title: Machine Learning-Based Prediction of Microbial Growth and Acidification in Yogurt Fermentation at Industrial Temperatures
Authors: Multiple authors (see ScienceDirect article)
Quote: Hybrid LSTM–SVR modeling accurately predicts microbial growth and acidification in yogurt fermentation, with prediction errors reduced by up to 86% versus traditional kinetic models across industrial temperatures.

Title: Systems Biology of Plant-Microbiome Interactions
Authors: Patricia A Rodriguez, Michael Rothballer, Soumitra Paul Chowdhury, Thomas Nussbaumer, Caroline Gutjahr, Pascal Falter-Braun
Quote: Systems biology approaches enable integration of diverse molecular data to understand how plants interact with and benefit from microbial communities through improved nutrient acquisition, growth acceleration, and stress resilience.

Title: Mathematical Model for Growth and Rifampicin-Dependent Killing Kinetics of Escherichia coli Cells
Authors: Researchers from Sabanci University and Dokuz Eylul University
Quote: The mathematical relationship between the concentration of rifampicin and the growth and killing kinetics of Escherichia coli cells is proposed using the solution form of a second-order linear differential equation for a damped oscillator applied to time-killing curve data.

Title: Agent-Based Modeling of Microbial Communities
Authors: Nagarajan, Karthik; Ni, Congjian; Lu, Ting
Quote: Agent-based models represent a class of powerful quantitative frameworks for investigating microbial communities because of their individualistic nature in describing cells, mechanistic characterization of molecular and cellular processes, and intrinsic ability to produce emergent system properties.

Title: Path-based reasoning for biomedical knowledge graphs with BioPathNet
Authors: Yue S, Zitnik M (Lead researchers)
Quote: BioPathNet is a graph neural network framework based on the neural Bellman–Ford network (NBFNet), addressing limitations of traditional representation-based learning methods through path-based reasoning for link prediction in biomedical knowledge graphs. Unlike node-embedding frameworks, BioPathNet learns representations between node pairs by considering all relations along paths.

Title: Innovations to culturing the uncultured microbial majority
Authors: William H Lewis, Guillaume Tahon, Patricia Geesink, Diana Z Sousa, Thijs J G Ettema
Quote: Despite the surge of microbial genome data, experimental testing is important to confirm inferences about the cell biology, ecological roles and evolution of microorganisms.

Title: Annotated dataset for deep-learning-based bacterial colony detection
Authors: Makrai, László; Fodróczy, Bettina; Nagy, Sára Ágnes; Czeiszing, Péter; Csabai, István; Szita, Géza; Solymosi, Norbert
Quote: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are probably the most efficient tools in this field, and there are efforts to use CNNs to automate colony counting.

Title: An open source knowledge graph ecosystem for the life sciences
Authors: Callahan TJ, Tripodi IJ, Stefanski AL, Cappelletti L, Taneja SB, Wyrwa JM, Matentzoglu NA, Reese J, Silverstein JC, Boyce RD, Malec SA, Unni DR, Joachimiak MP, Robinson PN, Mungall CJ, Gillenwater LA, Santangelo B, Vasilevsky NA, Bennett T
Quote: PheKnowLator (Phenotype Knowledge Translator) is a semantic ecosystem for automating the FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, and Reusable) construction of ontologically grounded knowledge graphs with fully customizable knowledge representation, including knowledge graph construction resources, benchmarks, and tools.

Title: Development of a novel cultivation technique for uncultured soil bacteria
Authors: Dhiraj Kumar Chaudhary, Altankhuu Khulan, Jaisoo Kim
Quote: The newly developed technique resulted in the successful cultivation of 35 previously uncultured strains, whereas no such strains were successfully cultivated by the traditional method. The use of soil collected in summer, a prolonged incubation period, and low-substrate modified media increased the recovery of uncultured and novel isolates.

Title: ACBM: An Integrated Agent and Constraint Based Modeling Framework for Simulation of Microbial Communities
Authors: Karimian, Emadoddin; Motamedian, Ehsan
Quote: ACBM models the cell population in three-dimensional space to predict spatial and temporal dynamics and metabolic interactions, providing improved predictions for microbial community dynamics.

Title: Gene products and processes contributing to lanthanide homeostasis and methanol metabolism in Methylorubrum extorquens AM1
Authors: Paula Roszczenko-Jasińska, Huong N Vu, Gabriel A Subuyuj, Ralph Valentine Crisostomo, James Cai, Nicholas F Lien, Erik J Clippard, Elena M Ayala, Richard T Ngo, Fauna Yarza, Justin P Wingett, Charumathi Raghuraman, Caitlin A Hoeber, Norma C Martinez-Gomez, Elizabeth Skovran
Quote: M. extorquens AM1 strains were grown in Methylobacterium PIPES media supplemented with succinate (15 mM) and/or methanol (125 mM) at 29°C

Title: Modeling bacterial growth and Allee effect via Allen-Cahn theoretical framework
Authors: Anna Maslovskaya, Christina Kuttler, Yixuan Shuai, Alexander Chebotarev, Andrey Kovtanyuk
Quote: The Allen-Cahn-based model of bacterial colony evolution combined with models of nutrient concentration changes and bacterial quorum sensing provides a theoretical justification for the modeling and in silico studies of the evolution of bacterial communities cultivated in nutrient medium.

Title: Generalized logistic model of bacterial growth
Authors: Anna Lo Grasso, Ada Fort, Fariba Fahmideh Mahdizadeh, Agnese Magnani, Chiara Mocenni
Quote: The work proposes a new mathematical model describing the dynamics of growing bacterial cultures as a first order non-linear differential equation as a generalization of the logistic equation, compared with the most studied mathematical models.

Title: Integrative systems biology approaches for analyzing microbiome dysbiosis and species interactions
Authors: Syed Sabih Ur Rehman, Muhammad Ibtisam Nasar, Cristina S Mesquita, Souhaila Al Khodor, Richard A Notebaart, Sascha Ott, Sunil Mundra, Ramesh P Arasardanam, Khalid Muhammad, Mohammad Tauqeer Alam
Quote: Multi-omics datasets, integrated with mathematical models, are used to reconstruct ecological and various types of metabolic networks in microbiomes...facilitating system-level understanding of microbiomes, providing insights into microbial interactions.

Title: KG-Hub—building and exchanging biological knowledge graphs
Authors: Caufield JH, Putman T, Schaper K, Unni DR, Hegde H, Callahan TJ, Cappelletti L, Moxon SAT, Ravanmehr V, Carbon S, Chan LE, Cortes K, Shefchek KA, Elsarboukh G, Balhoff J, Fontana T, Matentzoglu N, Bruskiewich RM, Thessen AE, Harris NL, Munoz-Torres MC, Haendel MA, Robinson PN, Joachimiak MP, Mungall CJ, Reese JT
Quote: Knowledge graphs (KGs) are a powerful approach for integrating heterogeneous data and making inferences in biology and many other domains. KG-Hub is a platform that enables standardized construction, exchange, and reuse of KGs with features including a simple, modular extract–transform–load pattern for producing graphs compliant with Biolink Model.

Title: textToKnowledgeGraph: Generation of Molecular Interaction Knowledge Graphs Using Large Language Models for Exploration in Cytoscape
Authors: James F, Pratt D, Churas C, Luna A
Quote: Large language models (LLMs) are trained on diverse literature, equipping them with contextual knowledge that enables more accurate information extraction, which overcomes limitations of traditional text-mining methods in capturing complex molecular relationships from scientific publications.

Title: Using design of experiments to guide genetic optimization of engineered metabolic pathways
Authors: Seonyun Moon, Anna Saboe, Michael J Smanski
Quote: Design of experiments (DoE) is a term used to describe the application of statistical approaches to interrogate the impact of many variables on the performance of a multivariate system. Definitive screening designs (DSDs) are a type of fractional factorial design that can estimate main effects as well as the interactions between factors

Title: gcMeta 2025: a global repository of metagenome-assembled genomes enabling cross-ecosystem microbial discovery and function research
Authors: Yan Sun, Qi Chen, Guomei Fan, Qinglan Sun, Qing Zhou, Jiaqing Zhang, Jingyi Nie, Juncai Ma, Linhuan Wu
Quote: By linking functional traits with microbial co-occurrence networks, gcMeta identifies keystone taxa central to biogeochemical cycling and environmental adaptation, and supports cross-ecosystem functional comparisons, revealing niche-specific metabolic pathways and stress-response genes.

Title: Metagenomics-Toolkit: the flexible and efficient cloud-based metagenomics workflow featuring machine learning-enabled resource allocation
Authors: Peter Belmann, Benedikt Osterholz, Nils Kleinbölting, Alfred Pühler, Andreas Schlüter, Alexander Sczyrba
Quote: The Metagenomics-Toolkit provides standard features such as quality control, assembly, binning, and annotation, along with unique capabilities including plasmid identification, recovery of unassembled microbial community members, and discovery of microbial interdependencies through dereplication, co-occurrence, and genome-scale metabolic modeling.

Title: Mathematical modelling of microbes: metabolism, gene expression and growth
Authors: Hidde de Jong, Stefano Casagranda, Nils Giordano, Eugenio Cinquemani, Delphine Ropers, Johannes Geiselmann, Jean-Luc Gouzé
Quote: A general framework for the kinetic modelling of microbial growth is derived from basic hypotheses about the underlying reaction systems, revealing how different modelling approaches are related at a deeper level.

Title: Lanthanide-Dependent Regulation of Methanol Oxidation Systems in Methylobacterium extorquens AM1 and Their Contribution to Methanol Growth
Authors: Huong N Vu, Gabriel A Subuyuj, Srividhya Vijayakumar, Nathan M Good, N Cecilia Martinez-Gomez, Elizabeth Skovran
Quote: 1 μM La resulted in maximal growth and culture density, while growth as low as 2.5 nM allowed growth at a reduced rate.

Title: Predicting microbial interactions with approaches based on flux balance analysis: an evaluation
Authors: Clémence Joseph, Haris Zafeiropoulos, Kristel Bernaerts, Karoline Faust
Quote: Flux balance analysis (FBA) predicts the optimal growth rate and its corresponding flux distribution for a specific medium, given a genome-scale metabolic model (GEM) of a microorganism

Title: Bacterial colony size growth estimation by deep learning
Authors: Nagy, Sára Ágnes; Makrai, László; Csabai, István; Tőzsér, Dóra; Szita, Géza; Solymosi, Norbert
Quote: CNN-based bacterial colony detection and the subsequent analysis of bacterial colony growth dynamics might become an accurate and efficient tool for bacteriological work and research.

Title: gapseq: informed prediction of bacterial metabolic pathways and reconstruction of accurate metabolic models
Authors: Johannes Zimmermann, Christoph Kaleta, Silvio Waschina
Quote: the novel Linear Programming (LP)-based gap-filling algorithm identifies and resolves gaps in order to enable biomass formation on a given medium, reducing gap-filling medium-specific effects on the final network structures

Title: Flux balance analysis-based metabolic modeling of microbial secondary metabolism: Current status and outlook
Authors: Sizhe Qiu, Aidong Yang, Hong Zeng
Quote: conventional FBA is tailored to simulate the growth and metabolism of microbes in the exponential phase, which is not ideal for simulating secondary metabolites' biosynthesis that typically occurs in the stationary phase

Title: A Microfluidics and Agent-Based Modeling Framework for Investigating Spatial Organization in Bacterial Colonies: The Case of Pseudomonas Aeruginosa and H1-Type VI Secretion Interactions
Authors: Wilmoth, Jared L.; Doak, Peter W.; Timm, Andrea; Halsted, Michelle; Anderson, John D.; Ginovart, Marta; Prats, Clara; Portell, Xavier; Retterer, Scott T.; Fuentes-Cabrera, Miguel
Quote: Agent-based modeling combined with microfluidics provides a framework for investigating how spatial organization in bacterial colonies emerges from individual cell behaviors and inter-cellular interactions.

Title: Culturing of Unculturable Subsurface Microbes: Natural Organic Carbon Source Fuels the Growth of Diverse and Distinct Bacteria From Groundwater
Authors: Xiaoqin Wu, Sarah Spencer, Sara Gushgari-Doyle, Mon Oo Yee, Jana Voriskova, Yifan Li, Eric J Alm, Romy Chakraborty
Quote: Complex carbon sources such as sediment dissolved organic matter and bacterial cell lysate are far more effective in enriching diverse and distinct bacteria from groundwater than simple carbon sources. Subsequent isolation from complex carbon enrichments yielded 228 bacterial isolates representing five phyla, 17 orders, and 56 distinct species.

Title: Hyperaccumulation of Gadolinium by Methylorubrum extorquens AM1 Reveals Impacts of Lanthanides on Cellular Processes Beyond Methylotrophy
Authors: Nathan M Good, Harvey D Lee, Emily R Hawker, Morgan Z Su, Assaf A Gilad, N Cecilia Martinez-Gomez
Quote: Gd was hyperaccumulated in an enlarged acidocalcisome-like compartment, constituting a 36-fold increase compared to wild type.

Title: Optimization of the fermentation media and growth conditions of Bacillus velezensis BHZ-29 using a Plackett–Burman design experiment combined with response surface methodology
Authors: YingWu Shi, XinXiang Niu, HongMei Yang, Ming Chu, Ning Wang, HuiFang Bao, FaQiang Zhan, Rong Yang, Kai Lou
Quote: A Plackett-Burman design experiment was conducted to determine the significant factors affecting the number of viable bacteria and antibacterial potency

Title: Systems Biology Approaches to Understand the Host-Microbiome Interactions in Neurodegenerative Diseases
Authors: Dorines Rosario, Jan Boren, Mathias Uhlen, Gordon Proctor, Dag Aarsland, Adil Mardinoglu, Saeed Shoaie
Quote: Systems Biology offers a holistic integrative approach to study the interplay between the different biologic systems as part of a whole

Title: MicrobiomeKG: bridging microbiome research and host health through knowledge graphs
Authors: Goetz SL, Glen AK, Glusman G
Quote: MicrobiomeKG is an integratively analyzable knowledge graph that was built from algorithmically generated knowledge assertions from supplementary tables of published microbiome papers. All edge predicates and node categories were standardized to Biolink ontology predicates and classes, and nodes were mapped to ontologies using compact universal resource identifiers (CURIEs).

Title: New Insights into Modelling Bacterial Growth with Reference to the Fish Pathogen Flavobacterium psychrophilum
Authors: Christopher D. Powell, Secundino López, James France
Quote: Two new models based upon the principles of Baranyi and co-workers using dampening functions to suppress potential growth, represented by a logistic and derived from rate:state differential equations, resulting in two newly derived growth equations: logistic × hyperbola and logistic × exponential.

Title: Mathematical Modeling for Fermentation Systems: A Case Study in Probiotic Beer Production
Authors: Ruarte et al.
Quote: Mathematical modeling of fermentation kinetics is a crucial tool to adequately describe and improve the performance of functional beer fermentation using probiotic bacterial and yeast strains.

Title: Optimization of a Bacterial Cultivation Medium via a Design-of-Experiment Approach in a Sartorius Ambr® 15 Fermentation Microbioreactor System
Authors: Antonio Baccante, Pasquale Petruccelli, Giovanni Saudino, Elena Ragnoni, Erik Johansson, Vito Di Cioccio, Kleanthis Mazarakis
Quote: A successful fermentation process depends on the synergy between an efficient bacterial strain and the formulation of a suitable growth medium. Design-of-experiment (DOE) combined with parallel microbioreactor systems enables advancements in fermentation process development

Title: Computational Metagenomics: State of the Art
Authors: Marco Antonio Pita-Galeana, Martin Ruhle, Lucía López-Vázquez, Guillermo de Anda-Jáuregui, Enrique Hernández-Lemus
Quote: The integration of various 'omics' data through bioinformatics approaches offers a more comprehensive understanding of bacterial physiology and ecology.

Title: A Practical Bioinformatics Workflow for Routine Analysis of Bacterial WGS Data
Authors: Aitor Atxaerandio-Landa, Ainhoa Arrieta-Gisasola, Lorena Laorden, Joseba Bikandi, Javier Garaizar, Irati Martinez-Malaxetxebarria, Ilargi Martinez-Ballesteros
Quote: The workflow supports all WGS data generated by means of the Illumina technology and performs genome annotation, species identification, serotype prediction, antimicrobial resistance prediction, virulence-related genes and plasmid replicon detection, core-genome-based or single nucleotide polymorphism (SNP)-based phylogenetic clustering and sequence typing.

Title: Agent Based Models of Polymicrobial Biofilms and the Microbiome—A Review
Authors: Koshy-Chenthittayil, Sherli; Archambault, Linda; Senthilkumar, Dhananjai; Laubenbacher, Reinhard; Mendes, Pedro; Dongari-Bagtzoglou, Anna
Quote: Agent-based modeling is described as a particularly useful computational approach to implement the various complex interactions in microbial communities when appropriately combined with an experimental approach, where each cell is represented as an autonomous agent with its own set of rules.
